column1: # Hox transcription factors — one tiny conserved domain in a sea of divergence.
# Hox proteins are master regulators of the body plan. Across paralog groups
# (PG1 anterior -> PG13 posterior) and clusters (A/B/C/D) they are highly
# divergent EXCEPT for the ~60-residue homeodomain, the DNA-binding helix-turn-
# helix that all of them share. In the alignment almost everything is gaps and
# mismatch while one block — the homeodomain — stays conserved; the InterProScan
# overlay marks exactly that block. The inferred tree tends to recover the
# anterior/posterior paralog-group gradient. No natural reference row.
# Columns: UniProt accession <TAB> row label (gene, ordered PG1 -> PG13)
